Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jan-10-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MOHAMMAD KHAN's Motion For Protective Order, as follows:

Defendant Mohammad Khan's motion for protective order "pending court determination of disqualification of opposing counsel" is denied. As Kahn reports, his motion to disqualify counsel was to be heard yesterday, January 9, 2020. Presumably the issue of whether opposing counsel is disqualified will thus have been determined. If it is not, Kahan may renew his motion for protective order.

Order signed.

Notice Of Motion And Motion Iso Motion To Disqualify Opposing Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT MOHAMMAD KHAN's Motion Iso Motion To Disqualify Opposing Counsel is a Duplicate calendar entry. (302)

Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Opposing Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-09-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael G. Thomas, Esq. of KERN SEGAL & MURRAY, T:415.474.1900, for defendant/moving party;
Heidi Machen, Esq. of MACHEN LAW, T:415.626.1140, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MOHAMMAD KHAN'S Motion To Disqualify Opposing Counsel, as follows:

Defendant Mohammad Iqbal Khan's motion to disqualify opposing counsel is denied. Defendant has not established an attorney-client relationship existed between himself and Ms. Machen, making him a former client. (See Koo v. Rubio's Restaurants, Inc. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 719, 728 ["Before an attorney may be disqualified from representing a party in litigation because his representation of that party is adverse to the interest of a current or former client, it must first be established that the party seeking the attorney's disqualification was or is 'represented' by the attorney in a manner giving rise to an attorney-client relationship."].) It is undisputed that Ms. Machen represented Gold Star Taxi LLC in various regulatory matters, including a dispute with SFMTA. (Machen Decl., 3, Exh. A.) Defendant claims to be a former member and 50% owner of that limited liability company. (Khan Decl., 2.) However, Defendant does not provide evidence of any agreement, express or implied, to represent him personally. (See Koo, 109 Cal.App.4th at 729 ["An attorney-client relationship is not created by the unilateral declaration of one party to the relationship."].) Representation of a partnership does not, by itself, create an attorney-client relationship with the individual partners. (Responsible Citizens v. Superior Court (1993) 16 Cal.App.4th 1717, 1731.) While it is true that Plaintiff in this action is suing Mr. Khan "dba Gold Star Taxi," and alleges that Mr. Khan engaged in business under the fictitious business name Gold Star Taxi (Compl. 2), only Gold Star Taxi LLC, not Mr. Khan, is Ms. Machen's former client, and therefore would have standing to move to disqualify her as counsel. (See also Great Lakes Construction, Inc. v. Burman (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 1347, 1356 ["Generally, before the disqualification of an attorney is proper, the complaining party must have or must have had an attorney-client relationship with that attorney."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT MOHAMMAD KHAN'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Mohammad Khan's motion to set aside the default entered against him on March 22, 2018 is granted. The default is now set aside and Mr. Khan must file an answer no later than September 7, 2018. Mr. Khan has presented sufficient evidence to support setting aside the default per CCP 473(b) and 473.5. Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and plaintiff San Francisco Independent Taxi Association has not argued, much less shown, that it would suffer legal prejudice by the granting of this motion. While the motion could have been filed earlier, Mr. Khan acted with sufficient diligence by having his counsel contact plaintiff's counsel approximately two months after Mr. Khan reviewed the complaint. Plaintiff's argument that Mr. Khan received notice of this lawsuit in sufficient time to respond to the complaint is based on inadmissible speculation and, even if accepted, would not preclude relief per CCP 473(b). The Court signs a formal order.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (302/HK)
